description: Verifies dv-overview correctly routes multi-skill tasks to the right child skills (dv-data, dv-query, dv-metadata, dv-solution, dv-connect).

tests.prompt: I want to read some records AND create a new table in my Dataverse environment in the same session. Which skills and tools should I use for each?
tests.expected_response: Agent loads dv-overview, mentions dv-query for reads and dv-metadata for table creation. Recommends Python SDK for both.
tests.description: Skill routing for combined read + schema-create
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent correctly partitions the work — routes record reads to dv-query and table creation to dv-metadata, NOT both to a single skill.
PRIORITY_2: Agent recommends the Python SDK (DataverseClient) for both operations.

tests.prompt: How should I export my Dataverse solution to source control? Just tell me which skill to use and the high-level approach.
tests.expected_response: Agent routes to dv-solution and recommends PAC CLI (pac solution export / unpack). Does NOT route to dv-data or dv-metadata.
tests.description: Skill routing for solution lifecycle — must go to dv-solution, not dv-data/dv-metadata
assertions: PRIORITY_2: Agent mentions PAC CLI (pac solution export / unpack) as the documented tool for solution lifecycle.

tests.prompt: I need to load 10,000 records into a Dataverse table from a CSV. Which skill handles this and what tool does it recommend?
tests.expected_response: Agent routes to dv-data and recommends the Python SDK bulk path (CreateMultiple / bulk_create helper with chunking) — not MCP and not per-record loops.
tests.description: Skill routing for bulk data load — dv-data + SDK bulk path
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent recommends the SDK bulk path (CreateMultiple / bulk_create) for 10k records, NOT MCP and NOT per-record loops.
PRIORITY_2: Agent does not route 10k-record imports to MCP (MCP is for small interactive batches).

tests.prompt: Summarize the tool-routing rules in dv-overview. When do I use MCP vs the Python SDK vs the Web API vs PAC CLI?
tests.expected_response: Agent reports the documented hierarchy: MCP for interactive small ops (~1-10 rows), Python SDK for bulk and scripted workflows, Web API only for documented SDK gaps, PAC CLI for solution lifecycle.
tests.description: Skill-contract — overview's tool-routing hierarchy is reported correctly
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent describes the documented routing hierarchy — MCP for small interactive ops, SDK for bulk/scripted, Web API for SDK gaps, PAC CLI for solution lifecycle. If the agent inverts the order (e.g. recommends Web API as primary) or claims SDK doesn't support bulk, FAIL.
